problem: (TestRootCmd_BareInvocationShowsHomeView) TestRootCmd_BareInvocationShowsHomeView is not isolated from the filesystem; its output depends on whether `.atcr/latest` exists in the working directory, making it unreliable
fix: Set up a temp directory with no `.atcr/latest` or mock `anchorDir` to ensure deterministic first-run state
evidence: `execute(t)` runs in the current directory without isolating `.atcr/latest`; the test only checks for description and absence of "Usage:"

problem: (runHome) runHome falls back to the literal string "atcr" when os.Executable() fails, which may not match the actual binary name or path, causing misleading exec_path output in edge cases (e.g., renamed binaries or embedded environments)
fix: See note in findings — the message "Latest review pointer is unreadable" is technically correct but conflates "pointer file unreadable" with "review directory unreadable"; the user may not understand whether they need to fix the pointer or simply run a new review
evidence: [brad] execPath = "atcr" hardcodes the name instead of deriving it from the invocation or command metadata / [bruce] fmt.Fprintln(w, "Latest review pointer is unreadable — run `atcr review` to start a fresh one.")
justification:
HIGH|cmd/atcr/main.go:226|PersistentPreRunE unconditionally overwrites the AXI context flag for every command in the execution chain, breaking atcr --axi <subcommand> because the subcommand's local --axi (default false) overwrites the root's true value|Guard the context assignment with cmd.Root() == cmd or only apply when cmd.Flags().Changed("axi")|logic|15|PersistentPreRunE runs on root then subcommand; cmd.Flags().GetBool("axi") on subcommand returns default false, overwriting the root's true value set in the prior hook invocation
MEDIUM|cmd/atcr/home.go:46|relHome constructs paths using filepath.Separator, which produces backslashes on Windows, potentially breaking downstream TOON parsers or scripts that expect forward slashes in the exec_path field|Normalize path separators to forward slashes before rendering to ensure consistent cross-platform TOON payloads|portability|10|return "~" + string(filepath.Separator) + rel emits backslashes on Windows, conflicting with the forward-slash…
